findings.quote: Because seeds must be grown out to a fresh crop from time to time before they lose the power to germinate.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: option C is a true passage fact but it is common to BOTH the capacity-matched and the outrunning sections, so it cannot explain the contrast the prompt asks about; it ignores the WHILE-the-outrunning-sections-did-not delimiter. Key D (it is the length of the wait for regeneration, not size in itself, that kills an accession) is the discriminating cause and is clearly best. Attractive but beatable.

findings.quote: That the pilot proved the current is bankable, so a utility can be promised a delivery schedule and held to it.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: option A verbatim restates only the bankable half of the Toft quote and is true, but the prompt asks what the DISTINCTION between bankable and servicing cheaply implies; A ignores the contrastive delimiter. Key D (a resource can be genuinely reliable while the project built on it stays too expensive to run) captures the implication of the distinction and is clearly best. Attractive but beatable.

findings.quote: Fäbodbruket rymde både ett verkligt naturvärde och stora umbäranden, men det var likväl riktigt att det till slut övergavs.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: the first half (both a real nature value and hardship) matches Halvardsson balanced view and is attractive, but the tail (that the abandonment was rightly done) is an unsupported normative claim the text never states and sits against her stress that the lost biodiversity kan inte aterskapas i efterhand. Key B is her verbatim varken forharliga eller avfarda stance and is clearly best. Beatable.
